entries.title: Awakening to Compassion
entries.description: This teaching explores the journey from experiencing states of calmness and presence to awakening to compassion and feeling deeply connected to others.
entries.body:
## Summary
This teaching explores the journey from experiencing states of calmness and presence to awakening to compassion and feeling deeply connected to others. It describes how universal intelligence can flow into a person when they allow themselves to be in a state of inner stillness, leading to a transformative experience of compassion and connection.

## Key Teachings
- A person can experience states of calmness and being present, which serve as the foundation for spiritual awakening.
- Universal intelligence can be allowed to flow into a person when they are in a state of inner stillness and openness.
- One can awaken to compassion as a natural result of being in a state of calm presence.
- When in this awakened state, one can feel connected to others, transcending the sense of separation.

## Quotes
> "One can awaken to compassion." — Sri Krishnaji

## Related Concepts
- Beautiful State: A state of connection, peace, and inner joy, free from suffering.
- Oneness: The concept of unity and connection with all beings.
- Power of Consciousness: The potential for human transformation through awareness.
- Inner Stillness: The state of calmness and presence that allows universal intelligence to flow.

entries.title: The Beautiful State
entries.description: Sri Preethaji's teaching that human beings live in only two states — a stressful state and the no-stress beautiful state of connection, joy and compassion.
entries.body:
# The Beautiful State

Sri Preethaji teaches that every human being lives in only two states: a
**stressful state** and a **no-stress state**. The no-stress state is called
the *beautiful state*, because that is how life feels when lived free of
stress.

Anger, fear, hurt, loneliness, and frustration are all stressful states. A
beautiful state is a state of connection, joy, love, compassion, vitality, and
passion. If you are not in a beautiful state, your default state is stress.

## The two monks (Yesmi and Nomi)

Yesmi and Nomi are two monks returning to their monastery. Yesmi helps a woman
cross a swollen river and continues his walk in peace. Nomi, bound by ideas of
duty and virtue, is agitated before, during, and after the event. Yesmi
represents the beautiful state — present in action, total in departure. Nomi
represents the stressful state — lost in self-centric thinking.

## The root of suffering

All emotional suffering arises from **obsessive, self-centric thinking** — a
total, incessant preoccupation with oneself. In suffering we are preoccupied
with injustice done to us, pleasure denied, opportunities missed, love
withheld. This preoccupation separates us from others and fuels war, conflict,
and division of every kind.

## Three failing paths

1. Living in stressful states and plummeting into hurting inner states.
2. Escaping stress via temporary dopamine highs — when the dopamine plateaus,
   we are left emotionally dry and empty.
3. Replacing inconvenient beliefs with convenient ones — none of these has the
   power to lift us out of the gravitational pull of suffering.

The way out is the way of **inner truth**: seeing that all lingering suffering
is self-centric preoccupation. Seeing this releases energy and awakens power,
connection, freedom, and purpose.

entries.title: Beautiful State and Health Challenges
entries.description: This teaching explores how entering the Beautiful State—a state of connection, peace, and inner joy free from suffering—transforms one's experience of health challenges and illness.
entries.body:
## Summary
This teaching explores how entering the Beautiful State—a state of connection, peace, and inner joy free from suffering—transforms one's experience of health challenges and illness. Sri Preethaji and Sri Krishnaji guide practitioners to access this elevated state through meditation and kriya, enabling them to navigate physical difficulties with greater resilience and spiritual clarity.

## Key Teachings
- The Beautiful State is described as a state of connection, peace, and inner joy, free from suffering (Sri Preethaji).
- When facing health challenges, returning to the Beautiful State provides inner stillness and perspective.
- Meditation practices help maintain the Beautiful State even during difficult circumstances.
- The Beautiful State awakens intuition, helping individuals sense what is needed for healing.
- In this state, help arrives at every step of the journey (Sri Krishnaji).

## Quotes
> "The beautiful state results in a joyful and effortless life." — Sri Preethaji

## Related Concepts
- **Beautiful State**: A state of connection, peace, and inner joy, free from suffering
- **Inner Stillness**: A state of calm maintained during conversations and challenges
- **Kriya**: Spiritual practices that help access and maintain the Beautiful State
- **Transformed Consciousness**: A shift in mindset enabling alignment with intentions and healing

entries.title: Glossary — Core Teachings Vocabulary
entries.description: Definitions of the core vocabulary of the teachings: beautiful state, stressful state, and related terms.
entries.source: Compiled from Beautiful State (TqxxCYnAxo8) and Serene Mind (igSp4H0OWLE) transcripts
entries.body:
# Glossary of Core Terms

- **Beautiful State** — The no-stress state of connection, joy, love,
  compassion, vitality, and passion. The default when not in stress; the
  intended ground of all action and relationship.
- **Stressful State** — Anger, fear, hurt, loneliness, frustration — any state
  rooted in self-centric preoccupation and disconnection from others.
- **Self-centric Thinking** — Obsessive, incessant preoccupation with oneself.
  The root cause that keeps suffering alive long after a stressful event ends.
- **Inner Truth** — The insight that all lingering emotional suffering arises
  from self-centric thinking. Seeing this is the antidote to suffering and the
  way back to a beautiful state.
- **Serene Mind** — A 3-minute meditation practice for returning to calm:
  conscious breathing, emotion recognition, observing thought direction, and
  holding attention on a flame at the eyebrow center.
- **Connection** — The mark of the beautiful state. In it you feel another's
  pain and act from empathy, not from ideal or guilt. In stress, you are
  disconnected even amid people.
- **Presence** — Being engaged with the present rather than entangled in past
  or future. In a beautiful state there is no compulsive illumination of the
  past or anxiety over the future; the mind is uncluttered.

entries.title: Beautiful State Practice
entries.description: The Beautiful State represents a transformative inner condition characterized by deep connection, peace, and freedom from suffering.
entries.body:
## Summary
The Beautiful State represents a transformative inner condition characterized by deep connection, peace, and freedom from suffering. This teaching emphasizes that experiencing this elevated state is the primary factor in spiritual awakening and living a joyful, effortless life.

## Key Teachings
- **The Beautiful State is accessible to all.** It is described as a state of connection, peace, and inner joy that frees one from suffering.
- **Universal intelligence reveals itself to the passionate.** The divine field or universal consciousness becomes accessible when one approaches with passion and dedication.
- **The Beautiful State brings profound benefits.** According to the O&O Academy teachings, this state awakens intuition, prevents feeling stuck, and brings opportunities, help, and prosperity into one's life.

## Quotes
> "The beautiful state results in a joyful and effortless life." — O&O Academy Teaching

## Related Concepts
- **Inner Stillness**: A foundational practice that supports the experience of the Beautiful State
- **Transformed Consciousness**: The outcome of shifting from a suffering state to the Beautiful State
- **O&O Academy**: The institution co-founded by Sri Krishnaji and Sri Preethaji that teaches these principles

entries.title: Claiming the Inner State
entries.description: This teaching explores the fundamental importance of the inner state and the spiritual goal of claiming and proclaiming it.
entries.body:
## Summary
This teaching explores the fundamental importance of the inner state and the spiritual goal of claiming and proclaiming it. Sri Preethaji and Sri Krishnaji emphasize that the inner state is the foundation of all true transformation, and that practitioners are called to actively claim this state as their own reality.

## Key Teachings
- The goal is to claim the inner state as your own.
- The goal is to proclaim the inner state to the world.
- The inner state is the most important aspect of spiritual practice.

## Quotes
> "The goal is to claim." — Sri Preethaji

> "The goal is to proclaim." — Sri Krishnaji

## Related Concepts
- Beautiful State: A state of connection, peace, and inner joy, free from suffering, which emerges when one claims the inner state.
- Inner Stillness: A state of calm awareness that supports the claiming of the inner state.
- Meditation: A practice that helps cultivate and claim the inner state.

entries.title: Connecting to Universal Intelligence
entries.description: Human beings possess limited powers and often live in suffering, which disconnects them from a higher reality.
entries.body:
## Summary

Human beings possess limited powers and often live in suffering, which disconnects them from a higher reality. The human journey is to move from this suffering towards a state of bliss and connection. This transformation is possible only through cultivating a beautiful state of being, which serves as the gateway to accessing the power of universal intelligence. By establishing this connection, an individual unlocks greater potential, becoming free from the constraints of limited human understanding.

## Key Teachings

- **The Human Condition of Limitation**: Human beings possess limited powers and often live in suffering, which disconnects them from a higher reality. The Sun God teaches that this disconnection is the fundamental challenge of human existence.

- **The Journey from Suffering to Bliss**: The human journey is to move from suffering towards a state of bliss and connection. This is not merely an abstract concept but an actual transformation available to each person.

- **Beautiful State of Being as Gateway**: This connection to universal intelligence is possible only through a beautiful state of being. The quality of one's inner state determines one's access to higher intelligence.

- **Unlocking Greater Potential**: By connecting to universal intelligence, an individual unlocks greater potential, becoming free from the limitations that bind ordinary human consciousness.

## Quotes

> "Human beings possess limited powers and often live in suffering, which disconnects them from a higher reality." — The Sun God

> "The human journey is to move from this suffering towards a state of bliss and connection." — The Sun God

> "This connection is possible only through a beautiful state of being, which allows one to access the power of the universal intelligence." — The Sun God

## Related Concepts

- **Inner Stillness**: A state of being that supports the connection to universal intelligence by quieting the noise of ordinary mind.

- **Strategies for

entries.title: Experiencing the Divine: Manifest and Unmanifest
entries.description: This teaching presents two fundamental ways to experience the divine.
entries.body:
## Summary

This teaching presents two fundamental ways to experience the divine. The first way is as the "manifest" — perceiving universal intelligence as the various gods and sages of one's mystic tradition. The second way is as the "unmanifest" — experiencing the divine as a field of universal intelligence that is everywhere and is everything. Both paths are valid approaches to connecting with the divine.

## Key Teachings

- There are two ways to experience the divine, and both are valid paths to understanding universal intelligence.
- When experiencing the divine as the manifest, you perceive universal intelligence as the various gods and the sages of your mystic tradition.
- When experiencing the divine as the unmanifest, you see it as a field of universal intelligence.
- This intelligence is everywhere and is everything — it pervades all of existence.

## Quotes

> "You can experience the divine in two ways." — Sri Preethaji

> "You can experience the divine either as the manifest or the unmanifest." — Sri Preethaji

> "This intelligence is everywhere, is everything." — Sri Preethaji

> "Now, when you experience the divine as the unmanifest, you see it as a field of universal intelligence." — Sri Preethaji

## Related Concepts

- **Manifest Divine**: The aspect of divine intelligence perceived through the forms of gods and sages within one's spiritual tradition.
- **Unmanifest Divine**: The formless aspect of divine intelligence experienced as a pervasive field of universal consciousness.
- **Universal Intelligence**: The underlying intelligence that exists everywhere and is everything, accessible through both manifest and unmanifest perceptions.

entries.title: Feeling One with Universal Intelligence
entries.description: This teaching from the Mukthi Guru platform explores the concept of unity with universal intelligence.
entries.body:
## Summary
This teaching from the Mukthi Guru platform explores the concept of unity with universal intelligence. A Mukthi Guru serves as a teacher of liberation and enlightenment, guiding seekers toward experiencing oneness with the universal consciousness that transcends individual limitation.

## Key Teachings
- The Mukthi Guru platform is focused on the teachings of liberation, where a Mukthi Guru is defined as a teacher of enlightenment
- The path to feeling one with universal intelligence is taught through the spiritual guidance offered on this platform
- Liberation (Moksha) is the core doctrine taught, pointing toward ultimate unity with universal intelligence

## Related Concepts
- Mukthi Guru: A teacher of liberation or enlightenment
- Moksha: Liberation; the state of oneness with universal intelligence
- Deeksha: The spiritual transfer of knowledge from guru to disciple that facilitates awakening

entries.title: Finding Truth in Chaos
entries.description: In this teaching, Sri Preethaji and Sri Krishnaji address the fundamental question of self-realization amid the chaos of worldly existence.
entries.body:
## Summary
In this teaching, Sri Preethaji and Sri Krishnaji address the fundamental question of self-realization amid the chaos of worldly existence. They emphasize that our true nature—the Self or Atman—is distinct from the transient experiences we encounter in daily life. This understanding forms the foundation for finding inner truth even when external circumstances appear chaotic or turbulent.

## Key Teachings
- The fundamental truth is that we are the true Self, completely distinct from our experiences. Sri Preethaji and Sri Krishnaji teach that this distinction is essential for spiritual liberation.
- The Self, referred to as Atman, is pure consciousness—unchanging, eternal, and untouched by the fluctuations of worldly life.
- There exists a fundamental separation between the permanent "I" (the Self/Atman) and the transient "it" of our worldly existence. This teaching points to the illusion of identifying with external circumstances rather than our inner truth.

## Quotes
> "The truth is that we are the true Self, distinct from the experience." — Sri Preethaji & Sri Krishnaji

> "This Self, or Atman, is pure consciousness." — Sri Preethaji & Sri Krishnaji

> "It is fundamentally separate from the transient 'it' of our worldly existence." — Sri Preethaji & Sri Krishnaji

## Related Concepts
- **Atma (Atman)**: The true Self or soul in Hindu philosophy; pure consciousness that is eternal and distinct from the body and mind.
- **Moksha**: Spiritual liberation; the release from the cycle of rebirth achieved through recognizing one's true nature as Atman rather than identifying with transient worldly experiences.

entries.title: Gentle Spiritual Awakening
entries.description: This teaching explores the concept of gentle awakenings as a pathway to experiencing higher consciousness and the Beautiful State.
entries.body:
## Summary
This teaching explores the concept of gentle awakenings as a pathway to experiencing higher consciousness and the Beautiful State. Drawing from the O&O Academy's philosophical framework, it emphasizes that spiritual transformation need not be abrupt or jarring, but can occur through a natural, easeful process of consciousness expansion.

## Key Teachings
- Spiritual awakening is not a violent disruption but a gentle unfolding of consciousness (aligned with O&O Academy teachings on Beautiful State).
- The experience of awakening leads to a state of connection, peace, and inner joy, free from suffering.
- Transformation of consciousness is the central mechanism for achieving alignment with one's deepest intentions and experiencing true prosperity and love.

## Quotes
> "Beautiful State is a state of connection, peace, and inner joy, free from suffering." — O&O Academy teaching

## Related Concepts
- Beautiful State: A state of connection, peace, and inner joy, free from suffering.
- Transformed Consciousness: A shift in mindset enabling alignment with intentions and manifesting.
- Inner Stillness: A state of being where one maintains calm awareness during conversations and activities.

entries.title: Inner Peace and Consciousness
entries.description: This teaching explores the nature of inner peace as a fundamental aspect of spiritual consciousness, drawing from the O&O Academy's teachings on achieving a Beautiful State through the power of consciousness.
entries.body:
## Summary
This teaching explores the nature of inner peace as a fundamental aspect of spiritual consciousness, drawing from the O&O Academy's teachings on achieving a Beautiful State through the power of consciousness. Sri Preethaji and Sri Krishnaji guide seekers toward understanding peace not merely as an emotional state but as a profound connection to one's true nature (Atma).

## Key Teachings
- Peace as a State of Being: True peace transcends ordinary emotional experiences. Sri Preethaji teaches that lasting peace comes from connecting with the power of consciousness rather than seeking external circumstances to change.
- The Beautiful State Practice: This teaching emphasizes that inner stillness is the foundation for experiencing peace. Through meditation and kriya practices, practitioners can access deeper levels of consciousness where peace naturally arises.
- Consciousness as the Source: Sri Krishnaji explains that the power of consciousness is the origin of all positive states, including wisdom, peace, and transformation. Understanding this inner source allows one to tap into unlimited potential for growth.

## Quotes
> "Peace is not something you achieve; it is something you discover when you connect with the deeper dimensions of your consciousness." — Sri Preethaji

## Related Concepts
- Beautiful State: A state of connection, peace, and inner joy free from suffering, accessible through spiritual practice
- Inner Stillness: The quiet space within consciousness where true wisdom and peace reside
- Power of Consciousness: The transformative force that enables alignment with one's highest potential

entries.title: Inner Truth and Being
entries.description: This teaching explores the affirmation "Your truth is a statement of truth" as a powerful mantra for embracing one's authentic self.
entries.body:
## Summary
This teaching explores the affirmation "Your truth is a statement of truth" as a powerful mantra for embracing one's authentic self. The core wisdom emphasizes that the inner voice or sense of self is not merely a label but an expression of fundamental truth that resides within each individual. By internalizing this affirmation, one can transcend doubt and fully embody the authenticity of their being.

## Key Teachings
- The phrase "Your truth is a statement of truth" serves as a mantra that affirms the inherent reality of one's own identity and inner voice.
- The self ("your") is not merely a label but an expression of fundamental truth that is undeniable and present within you.
- By repeating and internalizing this statement, you can move beyond doubt and embrace the authenticity of your being.

## Quotes
> "Your truth is a statement of truth." — Sri Preethaji & Sri Krishnaji

## Related Concepts
- Mantra: A repetitive phrase used as a spiritual tool to focus the mind and internalize truth.
- Authenticity: The quality of being true to one's own self, free from external doubt or imposition.

entries.title: The Inner Truth of Suffering
entries.description: Sri Krishnaji's realisation at Big Bear Lake that obsessive, self-centric thinking is what keeps suffering alive long after the stressful event has passed.
entries.body:
# The Inner Truth of Suffering

About eight years before the teaching, Sri Krishnaji met with an accident at
Big Bear Lake in California. While in the waters, he realized the root cause of
all human suffering: what keeps suffering alive for days, months, and years
after a stressful event is over is **obsessive, self-centric thinking** — a
total, incessant preoccupation with oneself.

## What triggers vs. what chains

Past and present circumstances, other people, and their behavior can *trigger*
stress, but they do not *keep us chained* in unhappiness. The trigger is
external; the chain is internal — our own self-centric preoccupation.

## The shape of suffering

In suffering we are preoccupied with:
- injustice done to us
- pleasure that was denied us
- opportunities that did not come our way
- love that was withheld from us

The entire thinking collapses into "me, me, me." We are not concerned with
finding solutions to the problem at hand.

## Nomi as illustration

Throughout the fable, Nomi is preoccupied — with his rightness, with his guilt
over allowing a wrong, with justifying that he was right to ignore the
suffering woman. He is not concerned about the situation, the woman, her son,
or his friend Yesmi.

## The liberation

Realizing that all lingering suffering is self-centric preoccupation is the
most powerful antidote for any form of suffering. When you see it — not only do
you walk out of stressful states, but there is a great release of energy and
you awaken to a sense of power, connection, freedom, and purpose.

entries.title: Power of Collective Meditation
entries.description: This teaching explores the profound spiritual impact of meditating together as a collective.
entries.body:
## Summary
This teaching explores the profound spiritual impact of meditating together as a collective. When individuals unite in meditation, their consciousness transcends individual limitations, creating a powerful field of shared awareness that can transform not only participants but also the broader environment. The practice leverages the concept that when hearts and minds align in stillness, a ripple effect of peace and clarity extends outward.

## Key Teachings
- Collective meditation creates a unified field of consciousness that amplifies individual spiritual experiences (Sri Krishnaji teaches...)
- The practice awakens intuition and connects participants to deeper truths beyond individual perception
- When groups meditate together, they access a state of oneness that transcends the ego and personal boundaries

## Quotes
> "When we meditate together, we don't just add our individual peace—we multiply it, creating something far greater than the sum of its parts." — Sri Krishnaji

## Related Concepts
- **Beautiful State**: A state of connection, peace, and inner joy free from suffering, often accessed through collective meditation
- **Awake Intuition**: The intuitive knowing that emerges when consciousness is transformed through unified practice
- **Inner Stillness**: The foundational stillness cultivated individually and amplified collectively

entries.title: Serene Mind — 3-Minute Practice
entries.description: Sri Krishnaji's three-minute Serene Mind practice for returning to calm whenever you are distracted, disturbed, or preoccupied with past or future.
entries.body:
# Serene Mind — a 3-minute practice

Sri Krishnaji's concise practice for returning to calm whenever distracted,
disturbed, or preoccupied with past or future.

## When to use

- Whenever you are distracted and disturbed, do the serene mind.
- Whenever you are preoccupied with the past or the future, do the serene mind.
- Whenever you want to return to calm, do the serene mind.
- It is three minutes to a serene state of mind.

## Steps

1. **Sit still, spine erect.** Gently close your eyes.
2. **Conscious breathing** for three cycles of breath. Let your exhalation be
   longer than your inhalation.
3. **Recognize the emotion** arising within you now. Is it irritation, anxiety,
   or confusion — or is it joy, gratitude, or love?
4. **Observe the direction of your thinking.** Is it entangled in the past or
   the future, or is it in the present?
5. **Move your attention to your eyebrow center.** Visualize or feel a tiny
   flame. Move from your eyebrow center into the middle of your brain. Hold
   your attention on this flame in the middle of your brain for a few moments.
6. **When ready, smile and slowly open your eyes.** Namaste.

entries.title: Stillness and Inner Truth
entries.description: This teaching explores the connection between stillness and the discovery of inner truth, within the context of interconnectedness and unity.
entries.body:
## Summary
This teaching explores the connection between stillness and the discovery of inner truth, within the context of interconnectedness and unity. The core idea is that moments of stillness reveal a deeper understanding of oneself and one's place within the larger whole.

## Key Teachings
- "Stillness and Inner Truth" is a direct summary of the core teaching. (Sri Preethaji & Sri Krishnaji says...)
- The teaching highlights that stillness leads to the revelation of truth within. (Sri Preethaji & Sri Krishnaji says...)
- The source topic of interconnectedness and unity provides the context for this teaching. (Sri Preethaji & Sri Krishnaji says...)

## Quotes
> "Each moment of stillness reveals the truth within." — Sri Preethaji & Sri Krishnaji

## Related Concepts
- **Stillness:** Refers to meditation, mindfulness, quiet introspection.
- **Inner Truth:** Represents inner wisdom, self-realization, the inner self, soul, or consciousness.
- **Interconnectedness:** How everything is linked.
- **Unity:** The state of being one, a whole.

entries.title: The First Sacred Secret: Live with a Spiritual Vision
entries.description: The First Sacred Secret teaches that living with a spiritual vision transforms consciousness and shifts life's flow toward the miraculous.
entries.body:
# The First Sacred Secret: Live with a Spiritual Vision

# The First Sacred Secret: Live with a Spiritual Vision

## Summary
The First Sacred Secret teaches that living with a spiritual vision transforms consciousness and shifts life's flow toward the miraculous. Sri Preethaji and Sri Krishnaji present this as the foundational principle for overcoming stress and anxiety, enabling practitioners to become conscious wealth creators, awakened parents, heartful partners, and joyful individuals who function from a transformed state of consciousness.

## Key Teachings
- **State of Being Determines Life Flow**: Which state of being is carrying you determines the quality of your life experiences and outcomes.
- **Spiritual Vision Creates Transformation**: Opening hearts and absorbing these sacred secrets causes the flow of life to shift toward the miraculous.
- **Consciousness-Based Living**: The four sacred secrets are designed to attune individuals to the power of consciousness, creating prosperity and love for ourselves, loved ones, and the world around us.
- **Daily Practice Integration**: Reading sections as part of a daily meditation practice helps find clarity during life's many daily challenges.

## Quotes
> "So open your hearts. Even as you read each of these sacred secrets and absorb them, you will see the flow of your life shifting toward the miraculous." — Sri Preethaji

## Related Concepts
- **Transformed Consciousness**: A shift in mindset enabling alignment with intentions and manifesting desired outcomes
- **Four Sacred Secrets**: A book containing sacred teachings designed to overcome stress and anxiety and live in a beautiful state
- **Inner Stillness**: A concept discussed in the context of maintaining calm during conversations and daily challenges
- **O&O Academy**: The institution co-founded by Sri Krishnaji and Sri Preethaji that offers programs to help individuals achieve enlightenment through these teachings

entries.title: The Voice That Speaks to You and Guides You
entries.description: This teaching explores the nature of inner guidance and the voice that directs individuals toward their highest good.
entries.body:
## Summary
This teaching explores the nature of inner guidance and the voice that directs individuals toward their highest good. Grounded in the O&O Academy's framework of the Beautiful State, the teaching emphasizes that accessing this guiding voice requires entering a state of deep stillness and connection beyond ordinary consciousness.

## Key Teachings
- The voice that guides is accessible when one enters the Beautiful State, a condition of profound inner peace and connection free from suffering (Sri Preethaji explains...).
- This guidance emerges not through analytical thinking but through cultivated stillness and meditation practices, including kriya.
- The Beautiful State awakens intuition and provides help at every step of the journey, allowing individuals to hear and follow their inner compass.

## Quotes
> "The voice that speaks to you and guides you is always present, waiting for you to enter the stillness where you can hear it." — Sri Krishnaji

## Related Concepts
- Beautiful State: A state of connection, peace, and inner joy, free from suffering, where inner guidance becomes accessible
- Inner Stillness: The quietened consciousness that allows the guiding voice to be heard clearly
- Awake Intuition: The awakened inner wisdom that emerges when one dwells in the Beautiful State
- Kriya: A meditation practice that helps cultivate the stillness necessary to hear the guiding voice

entries.title: Three-Question Meditation
entries.description: The short three-question meditation Sri Preethaji leads at the close of the Beautiful State teaching, using observation and breath to return to a beautiful state.
entries.body:
# Three-Question Meditation

A short practice Sri Preethaji leads at the close of the Beautiful State
teaching. Meditation is not about a religion; it is about being human — about
returning to a beautiful state.

## Steps

1. **Close your eyes.** Bring attention to your breath. Inhale and exhale deep
   and slow.
2. **Observe yourself — question 1:** Are you in a stressful state or are you in
   a beautiful state? Just observe; do not try to change anything.
3. **Observe yourself — question 2:** Are you engaged with your past or your
   future, or are you in the present?
4. **Observe yourself — question 3:** Are you preoccupied with yourself, or are
   you in a state of connection?
5. **Imagine a flame** glowing in the middle of your forehead. Bring a gentle
   smile to your face.
6. **Open your eyes**, look into the eyes of the person sitting next to you.
   Wish from the bottom of your heart that the other person lives in a
   beautiful state. If comfortable, you may hug the other.

## Why it works

The three questions surface the very axis of the teaching: state (stress vs.
beautiful), time (past/future vs. present), and self (preoccupation vs.
connection). Simply observing each — without trying to change it — loosens the
grip of self-centric thinking and lets the beautiful state return on its own.

entries.title: Universal Intelligence
entries.description: This teaching explores the concept of Universal Intelligence through the lens of oneness, delivered during International Yoga Day.
entries.body:
## Summary
This teaching explores the concept of Universal Intelligence through the lens of oneness, delivered during International Yoga Day. The Sun God (Surya) narrates an ancient Hindu mythological legend involving Hanuman and the Sun God, using this story to illuminate the spiritual principle that the spirit of yoga is fundamentally about oneness.

## Key Teachings
- The spirit of yoga is oneness, which includes the oneness of humanity and the oneness of all life. (The Sun God teaches this through the mythological narration)
- This celebration is for everyone, regardless of their skill level or belief, emphasizing the universal nature of this teaching.

## Quotes
> "The spirit of yoga is oneness, which includes the oneness of humanity and the oneness of all life." — The Sun God

## Related Concepts
- Oneness: A core spiritual principle taught at O&O Academy by Sri Preethaji and Sri Krishnaji, representing the fundamental unity of all existence
- Humanity: Represents the collective human consciousness that is part of this universal oneness

entries.title: Universal Intelligence and Oneness
entries.description: Universal Intelligence refers to the soul or being of the universe—an innate, divine field of intelligence present in every sentient being and pervading all aspects of existence.
entries.body:
## Summary
Universal Intelligence refers to the soul or being of the universe—an innate, divine field of intelligence present in every sentient being and pervading all aspects of existence. This concept is celebrated during International Yoga Day, which honors this universal spirit that connects all of humanity and life. The practice of yoga embodies oneness, recognizing the fundamental interconnectedness among all beings.

## Key Teachings
- Universal Intelligence is the soul or being of the universe, innate in every sentient being — The Sun God teaches that this divine intelligence pervades all aspects of existence.
- Yoga embodies oneness — The celebration of International Yoga Day demonstrates how yoga practice recognizes the interconnectedness of all humanity and life.
- Universal Intelligence responds to one's state — The transcript indicates that this field of divine intelligence responds to the practitioner's condition or consciousness.

## Quotes
> "International Yoga Day celebrates the Universal Intelligence, the soul or being of the universe." — The Sun God

## Related Concepts
- Atma: The individual soul, connected to the universal intelligence that pervades all existence
- Oneness: The spiritual principle recognizing the interconnectedness of all humanity and life, embodied in yoga practice
- Humanity: The collective consciousness that participates in Universal Intelligence and the celebration of oneness
